channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Harris will campaign in Texas to highlight state's abortion ban in a pitch to battleground voters
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris will head to reliably Republican Texas just 10 days before Election Day in an effort to refocus her campaign against former President Donald Trump on reproductive care, which Democrats see as a make-or-break issue this year.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Key battleground state voter registration data shows influential shifts favoring GOP
item.description: Voter registration data in Pennsylvania shows Democrats' edge over registered Republicans has fallen compared to 2020 in the key battleground state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Proposition 6 aims to ban forced prison labor in California
item.description: This November, California voters will have the chance to vote on whether to ban involuntary servitude in the state. Proposition 6, if passed by voters, would amend the state constitution to prohibit all forms of involuntary servitude, including forced prison labor.
item.dc:creator: Jacque Porter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump with slight edge over Harris in this crucial Western battleground: poll
item.description: With two weeks to go until Election Day on Nov. 5, a new poll in swing state Nevada indicates a margin-of-error race between Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vice President Kamala Harris to campaign in Houston ahead of Nov. 5 Election Day
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris is set to return to Houston on Friday, Oct. 25, for a last-minute campaign push before the general election on November 5.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Taylor, KPRC 2

item.title: Western Wisconsin race has strong implications for congressional control
item.description: Wisconsin’s third district makes up most of western Wisconsin. On the ballot this year, voters will choose between incumbent Republican Congressman Derrick Van Orden and Democrat Rebecca Cooke. The region has historically been a swing district the elected Barack Obama and later Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: In hard-fought Pennsylvania, fast-growing Hispanic communities present a test for Harris and Trump
item.description: ALLENTOWN, Pa. (AP) — The sun was creeping over the horizon on a recent morning in Pennsylvania’s erstwhile steel country, but inside a house next to two radio towers, Victor Martinez stood with a microphone, ready to broadcast his views to thousands of Spanish-speaking listeners.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia is behind viral disinformation targeting Walz, intelligence official says
item.description: A senior U.S. intelligence official says viral online content spreading baseless attacks on Democratic vice presidential nominee Tim Walz was created by Russia.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump selling 'MAGADonald's' shirts after drive-thru appearance
item.description: After Trump’s visit to the Bucks County franchise, McDonald’s sought to emphasize its political neutrality and said it does not endorse political candidates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: AP Decision Notes: What to expect in Arizona on Election Day
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Voters in Arizona have no shortage of competitive races to decide in the Nov. 5 general election, with control of the White House, the U.S. Senate and House and both chambers of the state Legislature in the balance.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT YOON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Project 2025's rightward vision became a flashpoint in this year's election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For the past year, Project 2025 has endured as a persistent force in the presidential election, its far-right proposals deployed by Democrats as shorthand for what Donald Trump would potentially do with a second term at the White House.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yellen says isolationism 'made America and the world worse off' in speech to global finance leaders
item.description: Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen says the U.S. economy has grown stronger because the Biden administration rejected isolationism, offering a barely veiled criticism of former President Donald Trump’s policies two weeks before the U.S. election.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump has made more than 100 threats to prosecute or punish perceived enemies
item.description: An NPR investigation has found that since 2022, Trump has repeatedly threatened to investigate, prosecute, jail or otherwise punish his political opponents, rivals and even private citizens.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach, MPR News

item.title: St. Paul’s proposed tax hike to fund child care: 5 questions, answered
item.description: A St. Paul ballot question next month asks voters if they’ll authorize the city to levy property taxes to fund child care for low income families up to $20 million over 10 years. The mayor says he won’t implement the tax hike even if voters approve it. Here’s what to know.
item.dc:creator: Kyra Miles, MPR News

item.title: Supervisor Leticia Perez calls out DA Cynthia Zimmer over investigation in explosive remarks during Board meeting
item.description: 17 News confirmed that a search warrant was issued, and investigators with the Kern County District Attorney's office raided her office in the County Administrative Building on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden is 'deeply concerned' about the release of secret documents on Israel's possible attack plans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is “deeply concerned” about the unauthorized release of classified documents on Israel's preparation for a potential retaliatory attack on Iran, a White House spokesman said Monday.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DeSantis using state money, time and his power to fight abortion rights measure
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — After a month of updating Floridians on hurricanes, Republican Gov. Ron DeSantis is now focusing his official office on fighting an abortion rights amendment, holding a campaign-like rally at state expense two weeks before the election.
item.dc:creator: BRENDAN FARRINGTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican lawsuits over overseas and military voting hit setbacks in 2 swing states
item.description: In Michigan and North Carolina, two Republican legal challenges to the legitimacy of ballots cast by U.S. citizens living abroad, including U.S. military members, hit setbacks Monday.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang, MPR News

item.title: AP Decision Notes: What to expect in North Carolina on Election Day
item.description: North Carolina voters will cast ballots for president and governor in the Nov. 5 general election while many in the state are still dealing with the aftermath and massive destruction caused by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Robert Yoon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Facing a budget deficit challenge, Johnson aims to safeguard youth and workforce
item.description: Enticing grocery store chains to open in underserved communities is another priority for the Johnson administration.
item.dc:creator: Julian Crews, WGN 9

item.title: Oregon elections office shuts down phone lines amid false info surrounding Trump, voters' pamphlet
item.description: The Oregon Elections Division is shutting down its phone lines after the system was "overwhelmed" by out-of-state callers responding to false information about why Donald Trump is not in the Oregon Voters' Pamphlet.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kustura, Fox 5

item.title: Former Minnesota Congressman Rick Nolan dies at age 80
item.description: Former U.S. Rep. Rick Nolan, who represented two of Minnesota’s congressional districts in Washington more than 30 years apart, has died at the age of 80.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff and Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Harris: Trump 'has no idea' about women's health care
item.description: “The man calls himself the father of IVF? I mean what does that even mean?” said Harris, referencing her rival’s comments on in vitro fertilization (IVF).
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Florida elections officials seek flexibility on voting rules after hurricanes
item.description: Florida's recent hurricanes could make it difficult for voters in affected areas to cast their ballots in the upcoming election unless Gov. Ron DeSantis waives and modifies some rules, county election supervisors said in a letter to state officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada political parties seek to sway ‘Gen Z’ amid inflation and recovery from an on-campus mass shooting
item.description: Young Democrats and College Republicans at UNLV are campaigning for their respective candidates in the 2024 election, citing issues such as inflation, gun violence, and reproductive freedoms, while the Trump campaign is targeting students through social media and face-to-face meetings.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5
